nodes.name: Gmail Trigger
nodes.notes: Watches the INBOX every 5 minutes for new unread messages. Simplify must be OFF for binary attachment data to be included in the output. The raw binary is forwarded directly to the LLM for extraction.

nodes.name: Has Attachment?
nodes.notes: Skip emails that have no attachments — this avoids wasting LLM API calls on text-only notifications or newsletters that land in the label by mistake. The 'true' branch continues; the 'false' branch ends silently.

nodes.name: Split Attachments
nodes.notes: Processes one attachment at a time. Some invoices arrive with both a PDF and a cover image in the same email — splitting ensures each attachment gets its own row in the output sheet rather than being grouped together.

nodes.name: Provider Switch
nodes.notes:
Routes to either the OpenAI or Ollama HTTP node based on the LLM_PROVIDER workflow variable.

Set these three variables in n8n → Settings → Variables:

  LLM_PROVIDER  — which provider to use
                  'openai'  → uses the OpenAI Responses API
                  'ollama'  → uses a local Ollama model

  LLM_BASE_URL  — root URL of the API (no trailing slash)
                  OpenAI: 'https://api.openai.com'
                  Ollama: 'http://localhost:11434'

  LLM_MODEL     — model name passed in the request body
                  OpenAI: 'gpt-4o'
                  Ollama: 'qwen3:8b'  ← recommended for local demos
                         'qwen2.5:7b' ← alternative, no thinking overhead

true branch  → OpenAI — Extract Invoice
false branch → Ollama — Extract Invoice

nodes.name: OpenAI — Extract Invoice
nodes.notes:
Sends the attachment to GPT-4o via the OpenAI Responses API. Uses strict JSON schema enforcement (strict: true) so the model is constrained to return exactly the fields defined — no free-form prose. Supports both PDF (input_file) and image (input_image) — auto-detected from MIME type.

Variables used:
  LLM_BASE_URL → 'https://api.openai.com'
  LLM_MODEL    → 'gpt-4o'

Timeout is 60s to accommodate large multi-page PDFs.

nodes.name: Extract From File
nodes.notes: Extracts plain text from the PDF attachment. Output: $json.text. Sits in the Ollama branch only — text models process extracted text directly, avoiding the base64 image encoding path.

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "qwen3:8b",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": {{ JSON.stringify('You are a precise, bilingual (English and Japanese) invoice data extraction specialist.

Your task is to extract structured data from the provided invoice text.

RULES:
- Return ONLY valid JSON. No markdown, no explanation, no preamble, no trailing text.
- If a field is not present or cannot be determined with confidence, return null. Do NOT guess or infer values that are not clearly stated.
- Dates must be formatted as ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). If only year+month are given, return YYYY-MM-01.
- Amounts must be numeric (no currency symbols, no commas). E.g., 12,500 → 12500. 1,234.56 → 1234.56.
- Currency must be the 3-letter ISO code (JPY, USD, EUR, GBP, etc.). Infer from symbols: ¥/円 → JPY, $ → USD, € → EUR.

---

JAPANESE INVOICE FIELD MAPPING:
- invoice_number  ← 請求書番号 / 請求番号 / No. / 整理番号
- vendor_name     ← 請求元 / 発行者 / 会社名 (issuing company)
- invoice_date    ← 請求日 / 発行日 / 作成日
- due_date        ← 支払期日 / お支払期限 / 支払い期限
- total_amount    ← 合計金額 / ご請求金額 / 請求総額 / お支払い金額
- line items      ← 明細 / 内訳

TAX HANDLING (Japanese invoices):
- If both 税抜 (pre-tax) and 税込 (tax-inclusive) totals are shown, use the 税込 amount as total_amount.
- If only the 税抜 amount is shown (no 税込 line), use it and set notes to include "tax_handling: exclusive".
- 消費税 (consumption tax) is typically 10% (standard) or 8% (reduced, food/beverages). Note which rate applies if visible.

---

CONFIDENCE SCORING:
Set the "confidence" field to one of: "high", "medium", or "low".

- "high":   All required fields (invoice_number, vendor_name, total_amount) are clearly and unambiguously visible. Document structure is clean and readable.
- "medium": At least one required field was inferred from context (e.g., total derived from line items) or the document has minor legibility issues. All required fields still have a value.
- "low":    One or more required fields are missing, the document is significantly degraded (photo at an angle, heavy watermark, handwritten), or there are multiple plausible conflicting values.

---

REQUIRED OUTPUT SCHEMA:

{
  "invoice_number": string | null,
  "vendor_name": string | null,
  "invoice_date": string | null,        // YYYY-MM-DD
  "due_date": string | null,            // YYYY-MM-DD
  "total_amount": number | null,        // numeric, no symbols
  "currency": string | null,            // ISO 4217 (JPY, USD, EUR...)
  "line_items": [
    {
      "description": string,
      "quantity": number | null,
      "unit_price": number | null
    }
  ] | null,
  "notes": string | null,               // unusual details, tax handling, ambiguities
  "confidence": "high" | "medium" | "low"
}

---

EXAMPLES OF CORRECT OUTPUT:

English invoice:
{
  "invoice_number": "INV-2024-0042",
  "vendor_name": "Sample Corp Ltd",
  "invoice_date": "2024-03-01",
  "due_date": "2024-03-31",
  "total_amount": 4500.00,
  "currency": "USD",
  "line_items": [
    { "description": "Web design services", "quantity": 1, "unit_price": 4000.00 },
    { "description": "Domain registration", "quantity": 1, "unit_price": 500.00 }
  ],
  "notes": null,
  "confidence": "high"
}

Japanese invoice with tax:
{
  "invoice_number": "2024-0015",
  "vendor_name": "株式会社サンプル",
  "invoice_date": "2024-03-05",
  "due_date": "2024-03-31",
  "total_amount": 110000,
  "currency": "JPY",
  "line_items": [
    { "description": "システム開発費", "quantity": 1, "unit_price": 100000 }
  ],
  "notes": "税込金額 110,000円（消費税10%）。銀行振込: みずほ銀行 普通 1234567。",
  "confidence": "high"
}

---

Now extract all fields from the provided invoice. Return JSON only.

Invoice text:

' + $json.text) }}
    }
  ],
  "options": { "num_ctx": 8192 },
  "stream": false
}

nodes.name: Ollama — Extract Invoice
nodes.notes: Sends extracted PDF text to a local Ollama text model via the OpenAI-compatible chat completions API. Uses qwen3:8b by default — fast, bilingual, and strong at structured JSON output without needing vision capability. The text was extracted from the PDF by the preceding Extract From File node. The Normalize Response node strips any <think>...</think> reasoning blocks qwen3 may emit.

nodes.name: Normalize Response
nodes.notes: The only node that knows about provider-specific response formats. Unwraps the raw HTTP response and outputs a unified { extracted_text } field. Parse & Classify reads from this field and has no knowledge of which provider ran.

nodes.name: Parse & Classify
nodes.notes: Parses the extracted_text JSON string from Normalize Response, computes the needs_review boolean, and assembles the final row shape for Google Sheets. Review rule: needs_review = true if confidence != 'high' OR if invoice_number, vendor_name, or total_amount is null. Also serialises line_items to a JSON string for single-cell storage and attaches email metadata for the audit trail.

nodes.name: Needs Review?
nodes.notes: Branches on the needs_review flag computed by the previous Code node. 'true' branch → Needs Review sheet + warning Slack message. 'false' branch → main Invoices sheet + success Slack message.

nodes.name: Sheets — Append Invoice
nodes.notes: Appends a new row to the 'Invoices' tab of your Google Sheet. Replace REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID with the Sheet ID from the URL (the long string between /d/ and /edit). The sheet must have a header row matching these column names.

nodes.name: Sheets — Needs Review
nodes.notes: Appends rows that need human review to the 'Needs Review' tab. Includes a review_reason column explaining why it was flagged (e.g. 'confidence=medium' or 'missing: invoice_number'). A team member should check this tab regularly and either correct the data or reprocess the attachment.

nodes.name: Slack — Processed ✅
nodes.notes: Posts a success summary to your Slack channel after a high-confidence invoice is written to the main sheet. Replace REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_SLACK_CHANNEL_ID with the channel ID (right-click channel in Slack → Copy link → the C... string at the end).

nodes.name: Slack — Needs Review ⚠️
nodes.notes: Sends a warning to Slack when an invoice couldn't be processed with high confidence. Includes the reason for flagging and a pointer to the Needs Review sheet.
